title: Wage, overtime, and meal-break law — Connecticut
target_keywords: connecticut wage overtime and meal break law 2026
primary_keyword: connecticut wage overtime and meal break law 2026

sources.title: Governor Lamont: Connecticut Minimum Wage Will Increase to $16.94 on January 1, 2026
Ogletree Deakins: Connecticut Minimum Wage Set to Increase to $16.94 on January 1, 2026
Conn. Gen. Stat. § 31-51ii — Meal periods. Exemptions. Regulations.
Conn. Gen. Stat. § 31-71b — Weekly payment of wages
CT DOL — Wage Payment Laws Guidance (updated December 2023)
CBIA — Jan. 1 Brings 3.6% Minimum Wage Increase
Nolo — Connecticut Laws for Tipped Employees (2026 rates: $8.23 hotel/restaurant, $6.38 bartender)
Day Pitney — Key Connecticut Employment Law Developments Employers Should Address in 2026
Littler — Warehouse Quota Notice Laws: Connecticut Joins the Club (SB 298, signed 3 March 2026, effective 1 July 2026)

high_concern_flags.detail: Row guidance gave 2026 minimum wage as $16.35; verified actual 2026 rate is $16.94 (Governor Lamont press release Sep 2025, Ogletree, CBIA, Day Pitney). Row guidance value was the 2025 figure. Resolved by primary-source override.

Row guidance described pay frequency as 'weekly minimum'; statute § 31-71b actually defaults to weekly OR every two weeks. Refined to bi-weekly-as-operational-default with semi-monthly requiring CT DOL approval.

pullquote_provenance.speaker: Tom Price-Daniel · Co-founder, Teamed
